factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, with a $23 million increase in payments for employee incentive pay.

evidence.text: Payments for employee incentive pay increased $23 million. Incentive pay is expensed in the year incurred and then paid in the first fiscal quarter of the following year.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing, leading to higher expenses.

factors.summary: The overall economic environment is a tailwind, with strong cash flow generation and access to credit markets supporting growth.

evidence.text: Our ability to generate significant cash flow from operating activities, coupled with our expected ability to access the credit markets, enables us to execute our growth strategies and return value to our shareowners.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong cash flow and credit access support growth.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is expected to be low-single digits across all segments, with Commercial Systems and Government Systems growing slightly, and Information Management Services growing at a high-single-digit pace.

evidence.text: •Commercial Systems 2016 revenue is expected to increase low-single digits when compared with 2015. [...] •Government Systems 2016 revenue is expected to be up low-single digits when compared to 2015. [...] •Information Management Services sales are expected to grow high-single digits when compared to 2015.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Growth is low to high-single digits across segments, indicating moderate performance.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly Information Management Services, which is growing faster than others.

evidence.text: •Information Management Services sales are expected to grow high-single digits when compared to 2015. [...] The increase in sales was driven by 7 percent growth in aviation-related sales, including GLOBALink SM and ARINCDirect SM.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher-margin segments is beneficial.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed with slight growth in some segments but a decrease in Government Systems due to market conditions.

evidence.text: During the first three months of 2016, total revenues decreased 5 percent, driven primarily by an 11 percent reduction in Government Systems revenue. [...] Air transport aviation electronics sales are expected to grow high-single digits, and business and regional aviation electronics sales are expected to decrease mid-single digits.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed demand with some growth and some decline across segments.

factors.summary: Restructuring actions are delivering measurable savings, with a $45 million pre-tax charge recorded in the prior quarter.

evidence.text: We recorded a $45 million pre-tax restructuring charge in the three months ended December 31, 2015 associated with these actions, consisting primarily of employee separation costs.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring actions are delivering measurable savings.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory is mixed, with some evidence indicating stability or improvement, particularly due to decreased cash usage for income taxes, while other factors suggest potential compression due to lower sales volumes and restructuring charges.

evidence.text: The decrease in cash used for income tax payments was primarily from the retroactive reinstatement of the Federal R&D tax credit as a result of the Protecting Americans from Tax Hikes Act.
Segment operating margins decreased 50 basis points to 20.1 percent primarily due to the lower sales volume.
The decrease in Government Systems operating earnings as a percent of sales was primarily due to lower sales volumes.

factors.summary: Input costs have shown a mixed trend, with a decrease in production inventory and other operating costs leading to lower cash payments, while raw material and component costs remain stable without significant changes impacting the current period.

Cost of sales consists of all costs incurred to design and manufacture our products and provide our services and includes R&D, raw material, labor, facility, product warranty, depreciation, amortization, service and support and other related expenses.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated positive forward guidance, with management both maintaining and raising expectations, reflecting confidence in future performance and the impact of beneficial tax credits.

evidence.text: The company expects this trend to continue in the future.

We are increasing our 2016 earnings per share guidance by 25 cents primarily due to the benefit of the Federal R&D Tax Credit, partially offset by higher restructuring and incentive compensation expense.
